paragraphs.body: Cold, silence, sterile. When you walk into a hospital those are the first things that you feel. Did those words make you feel a certain way? You can feel the pain and suffering that many have felt before you. For years, people have always wondered why doctors are so scary. The crazy thing is that some people are not even scared of them. This is shocking knowing a lot of people are usually scared, mostly kids. Why are doctors so scary? Many people fear visiting the doctor due to fears based on their age, past, and childhood experiences.

There are many reasons that one can find a doctor's office scary. In the article, Educate or Placate when Young Child is Afraid of Doctor's Visits, C.S. Mott Children’s Hospital explains the research on the fear of doctors in children. They prove their point by developing several different important ideas. First, they included details and research about a poll they conducted on children in doctors' offices. The article that was taken from a study showed different percentages of different occurrences in the doctor’s office. It was from getting to a shot to just being in the office. Next, they introduced a side of the parents of the children’s perspective. It stated how some parents must prepare their children before the doctor’s visit so they can relax during the appointment. They included how it has greatly impacted some of the results with anxiety and fear in children. Finally, they provided valuable information about the implications of young children having a big fear in doctor's offices. When children get used to the doctor, their anxiety and fear will eventually go down. It helps the child understand that doctors are not there to hurt them but to help them. It states how common it is to have a fear of doctors, to let people know they are not alone. I do to relate to this statement about being afraid of doctors.

paragraphs.body: The article provided some good points about how fear works and why some kids are affected by it. It stated, “The biggest culprit behind young children’s fear of doctor visits, according to parents, is that the child is scared of getting a shot. While “needle phobia” affects people of all ages, it can be especially tricky for younger children, when vaccinations are frequent” (C.S. Motts Childrens Hospital). They included this in their article because they are trying to show the reasons why children are afraid of doctors. The hospital did multiple polls to try and help their research to get an answer. So why are kids afraid of the doctors? Here is some of the research the article included about the polls they did. It included, “Among parents with a child afraid of doctor’s visits, fear of getting a shot (66%) and stranger anxiety (43%) were cited as the most common reasons children age 2-3 years were afraid. For children age 4-5, parents overwhelmingly said the most common reason was the child being scared of getting a shot (89%), followed by stranger anxiety (14%) and bad memories of being sick (13%)” (C.S. Motts Childrens Hospital). This information is trying to say that there are multiple reasons why someone would be afraid of the doctor. In my life I can agree that this is true of being scared.

annotations.comment: Is this information necessary for your essay's agenda? Let's talk about the "art of summary."

paragraphs.body: I have been afraid of doctor's offices all my life. It was difficult for me to stay calm among them whenever I was close to or stepped foot in their presence. When I was younger, I can still recall worrying about when my next well visit would be and attempting to avoid going. It was quite difficult to grow up fearing doctors when they were just attempting to help me. The main issue I had was with needles or shots. I had to get blood drawn frequently while I had Lymes disease, and it was unpleasant to experience this kind of fear. I used to tell myself it was all in my head and never really understood why I used to behave the way that I did. It simply never got around to processing. Needles still give me the creeps. As important as they are, hopefully I will not need them for long. In the future, I hope to move past it.

annotations.text: have been afraid of doctor's offices all my life. It was difficult for me to stay calm among them whenever I was close to or stepped foot in their presence. When I was younger, I can still recall worrying about when my next well visit would be and attempting to avoid going.
annotations.comment: Nice job transitioning into your own voice!

annotations.text: It simply never got around to processing.

paragraphs.body: I agree that the fear of doctors is true because my experience confirms it. I can only still imagine going and having to try to prepare myself to calm down. It never worked. The one thing about the different doctor's offices that always caught my eye was the different themes in the rooms. I can remember some had animals and some had several types of cartoons for younger kids. They always made the mood and stress ease a little because it was trying to paint a picture with the unusual colors and types of farm animals. Since I am older, I always wonder why I still do not like the doctors. Is it a fear of mine because I had it when I was younger? Is it because of needles? Is the doctors mean? Does a certain test involve blood work? Are all the questions that pop into your mind when thinking about doctors. For me I never like being sick but, every time I am sick, I hope it will go away. The problem is I would not want to get too sick and go to the hospital. Going there would make it worse and would make me not want to get treated.

annotations.text: Going there would make it worse
annotations.comment: Interesting. Why would going to the hospital make your illness worse?

annotations.text: Since I am older, I always wonder why I still do not like the doctors. Is it a fear of mine because I had it when I was younger? Is it because of needles? Is the doctors mean? Does a certain test involve blood work?

paragraphs.body: For these reasons, the fear of doctors is a common fear among people. Whether it is because of their age, past, or childhood experiences, it is still a problem to be recognized. Coming to realize and having information provided helps another feel not alone during the fright. Addressing fears and trying to overcome them will help a person feel not only better about themselves but improve their well-being. Again, doctors are not here to hurt you but only to help you.
